businessModel.text: Revenue from Cloud Services & Licensing (Oracle Cloud infrastructure and database, ~45%), licence support and updates (annuity, ~30%), and Hardware/Services (integrated systems, consulting, ~25%).

recentCatalysts.text:
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for AI: 3 new hyperscaler interconnect regions Q1 2025
    Cerner integration (acquired 2022): $800M synergy run-rate achieved, 15% cross-sell rate
    Oracle Database 23ai launched 2024 — vector-native capability for enterprise AI
    Microsoft Azure Oracle Interconnect: multi-cloud adoption expanding enterprise customer base
items: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for AI: 3 new hyperscaler interconnect regions Q1 2025
Cerner integration (acquired 2022): $800M synergy run-rate achieved, 15% cross-sell rate
Oracle Database 23ai launched 2024 — vector-native capability for enterprise AI
Microsoft Azure Oracle Interconnect: multi-cloud adoption expanding enterprise customer base

risks.risk: 1. Revenue growth 8% (FY24) lags hyperscalers AWS/Azure/GCP — competitive positioning risk
2. Autonomous Database and OCI still <20% of total revenue — transition incomplete
3. Cerner integration complexity: government health data migration projects running over budget
4. Acquisition of Sun Microsystems (2010) and Cerner debt still on balance sheet; leverage elevated
5. Oracle's governance structure: Larry Ellison's control through dual-class shares creates agency risk

entryExit.text:
Oracle (ORCL) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $127.04–$140.85 — current levels are fairly valued.
Trim / Take Profits above $140.85 — approaching the 52-week high at $345.72.
Stop-loss: A sustained close below $118.82 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
